subject: Mathematics
title: Euler: The Most Prolific Mathematician
description: A fast breakdown of Euler: The Most Prolific Mathematician for builders.

hook.text: Leonhard Euler published more mathematics than any person in history—over 800 papers and books.

buildup.text: He introduced the notation for pi, e, i, and the sigma symbol for summation.

discovery.text: His formula connecting e, pi, i, 1, and 0 is called the most beautiful equation in mathematics.

twist.text: He went completely blind in his later years and kept publishing—dictating from memory.

climax.text: Nearly every branch of math uses notation or results that Euler invented.

punchline.text: He went blind and still outpublished everyone.

quiz.question: What happened to Euler in his later years?
options: He went blind but continued publishing
